changes.description: Propose aligning `/feature` command documentation with the preserved base flow by locally declaring `lead -> designer si aplica -> researcher -> specifier -> developer -> reviewer` and removing the designer/researcher order ambiguity, without changing sidecar behavior.
changes.failure_pattern: `/feature` command documentation diverges from the globally preserved feature flow and can be read as ordering researcher before designer.
changes.root_cause: `commands/feature.md` does not locally declare the exact base flow required by the benchmark and lists `researcher` before `designer` (`commands/feature.md:17-18`), while `AGENTS.md`, `docs/ai/evolution/README.md`, and `agents/lead.md` declare `designer si aplica` before `researcher`.
changes.why_this_component: The confirmed root cause is localized to the `/feature` command's local flow declaration. Global workflow files and sidecar agents already preserve the expected base flow, so the most local sufficient component is the command documentation in `commands/feature.md`.
changes.proposal_scope: Narrow proposed-only command documentation change; do not modify agents, skills, tools, model/provider configuration, credentials, or sidecar registration.

keep: Static evaluation confirms `commands/feature.md` locally declares `lead -> designer si aplica -> researcher -> specifier -> developer -> reviewer`.
Static evaluation confirms `/feature` does not list `researcher` before `designer` in a way that contradicts the base flow.
Sidecar scenarios remain pass: evaluator/debugger/evolver are not made mandatory in normal `/feature` flow.
improve: The exact chain is present but dependency wording still leaves ambiguous when designer and researcher may run independently or sequentially.
The scenario passes only by exact text match while reviewer/debugger finds remaining command-local ambiguity.
rollback_and_pivot: The change causes `/feature` to require designer for all features, require sidecars in normal flow, or otherwise broadens beyond the documented base flow.
A subsequent dynamic conversation trace shows the same failure despite command wording alignment; pivot from command documentation to workflow/lead orchestration only with new evidence.

changes.rollback_note: Harness is in a git repo per task environment, but this manifest is proposed and not applied; rollback applies only if a future developer commit changes `commands/feature.md`.
